_meta.purpose: Belt faction definitions with named leadership, internal politics, and captain-facing leverage.
_meta.note: Post-collapse tone: dry, operational. Each faction has internal political splits; no single bloc voice. Bias-checked against BIAS_GUARDRAILS.md.

schema.leader: string — named leader with role note
schema.leverage: string — one sentence on what they control
schema.captain_angle: string — one sentence on how a captain can use them

factions.leader: Maren Cole — former T2 corridor controller, blacklisted after refusing a bribe
factions.internal_politics: The Network split after the corridor wars: one camp wants to unionize clearance priority through Corvallo, the other wants to burn the toll grid. Cole positions between them, paid by both to hold the line.
factions.leverage: They hold the corridor clearance codes T2 uses to prioritize haulers. A captain with those codes can bypass insurance blocks.
factions.captain_angle: The captain can broker a union vote, smuggle clearance codes to a rival captain, or sell Cole's ledger to T2 for standing.

factions.leader: Yara Vossi — salvage master, estranged from the Vossi corporate family
factions.internal_politics: The Yard is split between crews who sell only to genships and crews who take Trust contracts when the work dries up. Kade enforces 'no Trust' but turns a blind eye when a haul is too profitable.
factions.leverage: Pre-launch genship hull fragments and maintenance logs showing the 18-year fatigue failure.
factions.captain_angle: The captain can buy hull patches at discount, trade salvage data for safe passage through ED lanes, or expose the fatigue logs to trigger a fleet-wide inspection.

factions.leader: Anza Kowalski and Rafal Kowalski — co-leaders, competing claims on the same books
factions.internal_politics: Anza inherited the Kowalski maintenance tradition and wants to keep hull safety records public. Rafal inherited the billing accounts and wants to sell that data to T6 at a premium. They each control half the facility and do not speak.
factions.leverage: Verified genship maintenance logs and hull-certification chains that T6 uses to force mandatory refit billing.
factions.captain_angle: The captain can side with Anza to publish hull data to the fleet, side with Rafal to sell the ledger to T6 for credits, or broker a temporary truce between the cousins.
